title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovations #1979 through #1984

Family Table Recipe/Menu Cookbook
Credit-Backing Marks as trust accelerator for advance payment
Scheduled/pre-ordered meal pipeline (zero-waste restaurant prep)
Captain's Negotiation Toolkit (printable data-driven pitch tool)
founder_voice_quotes: The deeper your commitment, the more customers we send your way.

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovations #1985 through #1988
canonical_topic: Captain's Calling Card system with personalized QR-coded onboarding, sponsor-funded pre-loaded LB Cards, Durin's Door multi-path conditional access routing, and three-layer asynchronous onboarding package.

Sponsored LB pre-funded onboarding cards
Durin's Door conditional QR routing via secret phrases
Three-Layer Captain Pitch System (Plan + Card + Red Carpet)
Cue Card Generator with multi-transmission and scheduling
founder_voice_quotes: When you get a minute, scan this. It's made for you.

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovations #1989 through #1992
canonical_topic: Onboarding-triggered brand bounty system with six-tier rush pricing, reputation-gated designer pool with XP time rating, and cooperative demand aggregation for wholesale-priced creative services.

Six-Tier Rush Pricing (Good/Fast/Cheap triangle)
Designer Production Pool with 2-completion tryout gate
XP Time Rating (completion rate, quality, turnaround, volume)
Volume-Priced Cooperative Services via aggregated demand
founder_voice_quotes: You only get C+20 when you give C+20.

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovations #1993 through #1994
canonical_topic: Cooperative AI cost aggregation through shared Librarian infrastructure with institutional knowledge compounding that creates an inverse cost curve — per-member AI expense decreasing as membership grows while quality increases.
composing_primitives: Shared Librarian AI service layer (volume-purchased API)
Sipping Tea shared context caching model
Institutional knowledge compounding from member interactions
Inverse cost curve (per-member cost drops 99.4% at scale)
Cooperative AI moat via accumulated usage intelligence
founder_voice_quotes: Our AI costs DROP as we scale — the opposite of most SaaS platforms.

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovations #1995 through #1998

The Furnace authenticated cross-platform posting gateway
Shirley Temple Content Policy (confirm before send, not block)
Guru Unified Inbox (all platforms to one destination)
Screenshot Broadcasting across connected platforms

title: A&A FORMAL — Innovations #1999-#2000

Captain-aware conditional rendering (leadership tab)
Multi-entity global search fan-out
Type-tagged results with entity-specific adaptive cards
Cross-entity relevance ranking across heterogeneous types

title: A&A FORMAL — Innovations #2001-#2004

Business Scenario Research (save/ghost with 24h TTL)
Beacon Run Persistence Unlock (24h to 168h escalation)
Consecutive Share Chain Multiplier (+5% per share, 20% sustained cap)
share_chains table with streak and sustained_bonus tracking

title: A&A FORMAL — Innovations #2005-#2006

knowledge_links table with source/target/link_type
'Try it now' buttons from Cephas to live features
Feature pages 'Learn more' links to Cephas articles

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovations #2005 through #2006
canonical_topic: Cephas community-curated resource linking with reputation-only voting, tiered visibility thresholds (visible/verified/canonical/hidden) with natural decay, and bidirectional knowledge graph enabling gamified Beacon Run documentation exploration.
composing_primitives: Reputation-only voting (one vote, public, locked, no Credits spent)
Tiered link visibility with decay (1 vote per 90 days inactivity)
Ghost Rules for non-member link suggestions (24h TTL)
Bidirectional Cephas knowledge graph across all platform nodes
AI-suggested potential links via content similarity
founder_voice_quotes: This transforms Cephas from a static knowledge base into a community-curated, living reference network.

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovations #2010 through #2012
canonical_topic: Element-level design overlay via X-Ray mode (You Can Do Better) enabling member-proposed visual replacements, community-governed CSS Zen Garden themes with democratic voting, and multi-scale threshold governance for platform visual identity.
composing_primitives: Element-level design overlay (You Can Do Better on data-xray-id elements)
Community-Governed Visual Design (CSS Zen Garden model)
Design Democracy Voting (reputation-only for elements, credit-weighted for themes)
Portal-level independent theme governance (9 portals)
Fork-and-evolve seasonal theme system

title: A&A FORMAL — Innovations #2010-#2014

CSS Zen Garden community-governed visual design (Crown Jewel + Paper Candidate)
Design Democracy Voting (element/page/site stratification)

founder_voice_quotes: This is the paper. This is the Crown Jewel.

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovation #2013
canonical_topic: Four-layer hierarchical theme governance (Community Default → Guild → Tribe → Personal Override at cost) where override adoption rate serves as a built-in design quality feedback signal.
composing_primitives: Community Default theme (free democratic portal-wide baseline)
Guild/Crew Theme (professional group internal vote)
Tribe Theme (geographic/interest group vote)
Personal Override (costs Credits, Credits flow to theme creator)
Override adoption rate as governance feedback signal
founder_voice_quotes: The override rate IS the feedback mechanism. The cost IS the signal.

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovation #2014
canonical_topic: Guild and Tribe funded design contests using pooled cooperative currency (Credits) treasury for visual identity competitions — banners, themes, mascots, icons — with internal democratic voting and winner receiving pooled Credits.
composing_primitives: Guild/Tribe Collective Credit contest treasury (voluntary contributions)
Document Voting (reputation-only) for internal contest governance
Contest Pipeline (K120) reuse for visual identity
Design Pipeline reputation progression for contest winners
Tiered Theme Governance integration (Layer 2 Guild / Layer 3 Tribe)
founder_voice_quotes: The Guild doesn't hire a designer — it invites its community to participate in defining its visual identity.

title: Acknowledgment & Assignment — Innovations #2015 through #2019

Tribe Mirror with geography/interest/cultural/hybrid types

title: A&A FORMAL — Innovations #2022–#2039

title: A&A FORMAL — Innovation #2022

60° twist-lock sleeve clamping system
Gravity + vacuum-assist dual-pressure (no hydraulics required)

Universal canister diameters (S/M/L/XL) accepting 3D-printed inserts
founder_voice_quotes: This turns every member with a 3D printer into a potential injection mold operator.
